expressions.english: good friend
expressions.notes:
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
좋은 is the adjective form of 좋다.

expressions.english: bad friend
expressions.notes:
나쁘다 = to be bad
나쁜 is the adjective form of 나쁘다.

expressions.english: close friend
expressions.notes:
친하다 = to be close (with other people)
친한 is the adjective form of 친하다.

expressions.english: friend from school
to play with a friend
expressions.notes: 놀다 = to play; to hang out

expressions.english: to become friends
to make a friend
expressions.notes: 사귀다 = to make friends; to date someone

expressions.english: to fight with a friend
This is my friend.
It's okay because we are friends.
expressions.notes: 괜찮다 = to be okay; to be alright; to be nice

expressions.english: I am going to meet my friend.
expressions.notes:
만나다 = to meet (up)
-(으)ㄹ 거야 = will; be going to + V

expressions.english: Please add me as a friend.
My friend gave it to me as a gift.
My friend and I lost touch.
expressions.notes:
연락 = contact
끊기다 = to be cut off; to be disconnected

expressions.english: I'll ask my friend.
expressions.notes:
물어보다 = to ask
-(으)ㄹ게요 = I promise I will + V; I will + V

expressions.english: I slept over at my friend's house yesterday.
I miss my friend who has gone to study abroad.
expressions.notes:
유학 = studying abroad
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.english: Yesterday, I met my friends from my hometown.

expressions.korean: 친구들 중에서 제가 제일 키가 커요.
expressions.english: I'm the tallest among my friends.
expressions.notes:
중(에서) = among; out of; between; in
제일 = the most + adjective/adverb
키 = height
크다 = to be big; to be tall; to be loud

expressions.english: Whenever I meet my friends, we always chatter.
expressions.notes:
수다 = chatter
수다를 떨다 = to chatter
